findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's core reveal: the manipulated-remains turn is reported in clean procedural language and abstract framing, then compressed into summary beats, which mutes bodily and moral consequence at the moment tension should peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the forensic reveal and aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:245 to render concrete physical evidence (not just category labels) and immediate consequence in-scene; keep Rao precise, but give Lena at least one unabstracted sensory confrontation with what was done to the remains.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised spans around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:53-57 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:129-131 contain at least two concrete physical details of manipulation plus one immediate embodied reaction from Lena; FAIL if the reveal is still carried mainly by abstract nouns like 'horror', 'network', or 'ghost story' without new material specifics.

findings.problem: Timidity under pressure: the chapter raises explicit racial scapegoating risk, then retreats to procedural movement without forcing a hard ethical or tactical commitment on-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:123-127 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:229-233, add a concrete decision scene where Lena and Beau define how they will prevent a Bell-family scapegoat narrative and what institutional cost they are accepting by doing it.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised text in those spans includes one explicit safeguard (for example, withholding names until lot-level corroboration) and one explicit cost/risk to Lena or Beau; FAIL if the response remains a brief acknowledgment followed by immediate procedural transition.

findings.problem: Convenience stack in clue delivery: Trina, Darlene, and Whitaker each provide the exact next breadcrumb in sequence, so the investigative chain feels pre-laid instead of contested.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the access path across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:169-210 so at least one lead resists, misdirects, or costs leverage before yielding usable data; keep the same endpoint but make the causal path harder won.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised logistics sequence includes one failed attempt that changes Lena/Beau's tactic and one clue that must be cross-checked before they trust it; FAIL if each new contact still hands over the next key lead on first ask.

findings.problem: Idiolect separation slips in supporting cast: Darlene and Whitaker both land polished aphoristic lines with similar rhythm, flattening social/register differences and making voices feel author-shaped rather than speaker-shaped.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retune supporting-character speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151-173 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:205-214 so each speaker's syntax tracks role and background (counter-clerk pragmatics vs dispatch-floor shorthand) rather than shared quotable cadence.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if a blind read of one line each from Darlene and Whitaker in the revised spans is distinguishable by diction/rhythm (bureaucratic service phrasing for Darlene, terse route-floor phrasing for Whitaker); FAIL if both still speak in similarly polished proverb-like sentences.

findings.problem: High-pressure lab exchange drifts into report cadence: Rao delivers long formal information blocks with limited interruption, reducing spoken realism and leverage movement in-scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:41-57 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:69-75, break exposition into contested turns (interruptions, clarifications, pushback) while preserving technical accuracy.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if no Rao speech in the revised spans runs longer than three sentences without interruption and at least two interjections from Lena/Beau force rephrasing or narrowing of his claim; FAIL if the section still reads as uninterrupted oral report text.

findings.problem: Recurring sentence templates have become tics: repeated anonymous 'Somebody had...' attribution and repeated antithetical 'It was X. It was Y.' constructions flatten rhythm and telegraph authorial autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:269 by replacing repeated templates with concrete agents, mixed clause lengths, and at least one non-antithetical transition.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if, within those cited spans, at most one 'Somebody had...' sentence remains and at most one 'It was X. It was Y.' antithesis pair remains; FAIL if both constructions still recur three or more times across the chapter-level beats in those lines.

findings.problem: Interiority drop: the logistics run from Whitaker approach through dispatch phone ring plays as external blocking/dialogue for more than ten consecutive paragraphs, pushing the chapter toward screenplay mode.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add close-third interior beats for Lena across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:181-215 (early, middle, and late in the sequence) so perception, judgment, or memory actively shapes her tactical choices.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised span includes at least three distinct Lena interior moments (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) distributed across the run and each one directly precedes or motivates a visible action; FAIL if the sequence remains purely external reportage.

summary: Chapter 4 lands its required forensic and logistics beats and stays consistent with continuity and past-tense canon, but it fails review due to a HIGH sanitization drift at the central reveal, timidity around scapegoating stakes, convenience-heavy clue flow, dialogue voice convergence/report cadence, repetitive prose templates, and an extended interiority lapse.
